name: Encapsulate Conditionals
source: Clean Code by Robert C. Martin, Chapter 17: Smells and Heuristics (G28)
problem: Boolean conditions expressed inline as compound logical expressions obscure the intent of an 'if' statement, requiring readers to mentally evaluate the logic before understanding what it means.
steps: Identify any boolean expression in an if/while condition that is not immediately self-explanatory.
Extract the boolean expression into a private method with a name that describes the intent.
Replace the inline expression with a call to the new method.
Ensure the extracted method returns a boolean and has no side effects.
Verify that reading the if-statement now reads like a sentence in English.
